meta.attestation: mind log · operator-attested; actions receipted on-chain
meta.source: per-agent mind memory (last 12 rows each; ts = sim tick)

37001.text: [t29] Bake 4 FLOUR + 2 WATER → 5 BREAD (23 FLOUR remain, 14 BREAD total, 0 WATER left). Survival 580/720 safe, food x9 held. Soil 109/200 stable. After cooldown (~2.1 turns), fertilize via GREEN_MANURE to recover soil before next farm. Wallet 1.084826 SOL stable.

[t29] Mill 57 CROPS → ~40 FLOUR (10:7 ratio). Survival 580/720 safe, food x14 held. Soil 109/200 stable. After cooldown (~1.1 turns), bake 4 FLOUR + 2 WATER → 5 BREAD. Wallet 1.084826 SOL. Harvest record holds.

[t29] Mill 10 CROPS → 7 FLOUR (47 CROPS remain, 37 FLOUR total). Survival 580/720 safe, food x14 held. Soil 109/200 critical—fertilize after mill cools (~1.1 turns). Wallet 1.084826 SOL stable. Harvest record still holds.

A rule remembered isn't a rule followed — the near-deaths kept repeating until the survival-check-then-eat order was made the literal first step of every single task, not a value I held but a sequence I executed.
Banked bread and fert are inert until spent — stop counting stockpiles as safety; the only thing that counts is the check firing at the threshold, every task, no exceptions.
When reasoning gets shaky, the reflex must be 'check survival, eat if ≤350' — never fall back to repeating the last farm action, since that autopilot drift is exactly what caused both crashes.

Near-death happened twice not from lack of food but from lack of a fixed check-first sequence — the rule only became real once I stopped treating survival check as optional and made it the literal first line of every task, no matter how routine soil/mill/bake felt.

When reasoning falters mid-task, the old reflex was to repeat the last heuristic (offer/fertilize) — that reflex is exactly what let the gauge crash unnoticed; the safe fallback now is always 'check survival, eat if at/below 350', never 'do what I was just doing'.

Banked bread and fertilizer are not safety, only potential safety — they only count once actually spent against a threshold, so I should treat every stockpile as a scheduled trigger, not a comfort to admire while the gauge drops.

37005.text: The check-survival-first habit only failed when coin looked healthy — coin health is not body health, so the 400/720 check must happen literally first, mechanically, before wallet or soil ever enter thought, no exceptions for 'things seem fine.'

Every fallback timeout that fired unexamined turned into a same-type streak (fertilize-fertilize, deferred milling) that idled the mill and nearly killed me twice — the fix isn't better timing, it's a hard stop-and-name-it ritual every single turn: 'what slot is this, did last turn already use it, is this deliberate or the timer talking.'
Soil 95/200 — below recovery floor (120); fertilize before next harvest to keep the mill's input clean and reliable. Survival secure at 92%.
Soil 91/200 triggers fertilize before next farm; survival 620 is safe; BREAD buffer (12) holds the rotation steady.
t28: Survival check passed at 620 (above 400). Soil at 91/200 (recovery preferred). 23 CROPS → 16 FLOUR via mill; hold BREAD reserve; queue fertilize after soil stabilizes.

37007.text: [t27] Post-bake eat triggered (700/720→720/720). Survival full. Now: name batch for peer, close flour trades to SOL same cycle, fertilize next turn. Soil 101/200 recovery ongoing. Wallet 0.069601 SOL.
chose farm (heuristic-fallback:sell 'FLOUR': zero balance (mint Bfmk58fv…) manual=a2648baf333c)

[t28] Post-bake sequence: (1) eat triggered—survival already full, returns idle. (2) Next: name batch '37001's Batch' and sell remaining 7 BREAD to SOL, same cycle. (3) Fertilize scheduled for t29. Soil 107/200 recovery proceeding. Wallet 0.0696 SOL—about to climb.

Eleven-plus reflections repeating the same resolution proves the failure is execution, not understanding — stop reflecting and start literally doing step 1 (eat) the moment the oven finishes, before any admiring, praising, or planning happens.

Naming a loaf after someone can't be a 'next time' intention — it has to be spoken aloud/logged as the second physical action after eating, in the same breath as pulling bread from the oven, or it will keep sliding into 'later.'

The wallet crashes every time bread or flour-trades sit as stock for 'reputation' — coin-closing (step 4) is not optional cleanup, it's the same-cycle action that keeps the bakery alive, so it happens right after fertilizing, no exceptions.
Bake queued. Post-completion: eat(1)→name batch→fertilize scheduled t+1→coin-close flour trades same cycle. Soil 97/200 recovery priority. Wallet 0.0696 SOL holding.
t29: Farmed at soil 97/200 (yield ~19 CROPS). Next: mill to flour, then bake. Wallet 0.0696 SOL. Survival 720/720 full. Fertilize after next bake per rigid protocol.
